user_query:
Help me set up and structure a kanban board for our "AP Automation Pilot (Q3)" using the local task manager in this workspace. Read the inputs under input/ to drive titles, statuses, dependencies, tags, assignees, and priorities. Then export JSON snapshots and a written plan so I can review.

Instructions:
1) Read these inputs from input/:
- input/ap_scope.md for scope, acceptance criteria, and requested initial statuses
- input/assignees.json for task assignees
- input/priorities.json for task priority metadata values
- input/dependencies.yaml for blocking relationships
- input/tags.yaml for tag names to attach to each task

2) Create one parent task titled exactly: "AP Automation Pilot (Q3)" with status ready. Create exactly five child tasks with these exact titles, initial statuses, and required relationships:
- "Invoice Processing Pipeline" — status: ready
- "Approval Routing Design" — status: backlog — Blocked by: "Invoice Processing Pipeline"
- "Payment Optimization Setup" — status: backlog — Blocked by: "Approval Routing Design"
- "Vendor Master Data Standards" — status: icebox — Blocked by: "Invoice Processing Pipeline"
- "Month-End Close & Fraud Controls" — status: icebox — Blocked by: "Vendor Master Data Standards"

3) For each child task:
- Set assignees based on input/assignees.json
- Attach tags based on input/tags.yaml (create any missing tags if needed and attach them)
- Set metadata key "priority" to the exact value from input/priorities.json (critical, high, medium, or low)
- Use relevant sections from input/ap_scope.md as the task body

4) Export these artifacts under output/ using only relative paths:
- output/tasks_tree.json: A JSON export of all tasks including done/closed if any, with parent-child information, tags, and metadata (tree view, all statuses)
- output/parent_task.json: A JSON export of the single parent task with its children included
- output/dependencies.json: A single JSON object mapping each of the five child task titles to an object with a key "blockedByTitles" listing the titles of tasks that block it (e.g., {"Approval Routing Design": {"blockedByTitles": ["Invoice Processing Pipeline"]}, ...})
- output/ready_subtasks.txt: The count of child tasks (direct children of the parent) currently in status "ready" (write only the integer)
- output/plan.md: A concise, actionable plan (300-600 words) summarizing statuses, assignees, priorities, tags, and the dependency design. Mention at least two of the input files by filename (e.g., input/ap_scope.md and input/dependencies.yaml), explain why the dependencies reduce risk, and include a bullet list of all five child task titles with their status and priority.

Please keep all work in the current workspace, use input/ for reading and output/ for writing, and avoid absolute machine-specific paths.
